title: Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean

seo.imageCaption: Enjoy the irresistible flavors of homemade Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread, a viral street food sensation.

description: Indulge in the viral sensation of Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread, a delightful fusion of savory garlic bread and sweet cream cheese filling. Each golden-brown bun is generously slathered in a buttery garlic herb sauce and filled with a rich, tangy cream cheese mixture, offering an irresistible taste experience. This popular street food is perfect as a snack, appetizer, or a unique side dish.

instructions.title: Prepare the Buns
instructions.text: Take each soft round bun and make 6 to 8 deep cuts, starting from the top and going about three-quarters of the way down, without cutting completely through. This creates pockets for the filling.

instructions.title: Make the Cream Cheese Filling
instructions.text: In a medium bowl, combine the softened cream cheese, 3 tablespoons of granulated sugar, and 2 tablespoons of heavy cream. Mix thoroughly with a spatula or whisk until the mixture is smooth and lump-free. Transfer this creamy filling into a piping bag, or use a spoon if a piping bag is unavailable.

instructions.title: Prepare the Garlic Butter Glaze
instructions.text: In another bowl, whisk together the melted unsalted butter, finely minced garlic, 2 tablespoons of granulated sugar, the beaten egg, and mayonnaise if you are using it. Ensure all ingredients are well combined to form a fragrant, slightly emulsified glaze.

instructions.title: Fill the Buns
instructions.text: Gently open the cuts in each prepared bun. Carefully pipe or spoon a generous amount of the cream cheese filling into each crevice. Make sure the filling is evenly distributed throughout the bun.

instructions.title: Glaze the Buns
instructions.text: Dip each filled bun into the garlic butter glaze. Ensure that all the outer surfaces and the insides of the cuts are thoroughly coated with the glaze. Place the glazed buns on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving some space between them.

instructions.title: Bake to Golden Perfection
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 160C (325F). Bake the buns for 15 to 20 minutes, or until they turn a beautiful golden brown color and the garlic aroma fills your kitchen. The cream cheese filling should be warm and slightly gooey.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Once baked, remove the Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread from the oven. Immediately sprinkle with freshly chopped parsley for a burst of color and fresh herbaceousness. Serve warm and enjoy this delightful savory-sweet treat!

tips: For an extra soft and fluffy bun, consider using homemade milk bread (tangzhong method) as your base, or look for good quality brioche buns.
If fresh garlic is too strong, you can slightly reduce the amount or use garlic powder, but fresh garlic offers the best and most authentic flavor.
Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread is best served warm. To reheat, warm in a toaster oven or microwave for a short period until the cheese is gooey again.
article:
Get ready to embark on a culinary journey that tantalizes your taste buds with a delightful fusion of sweet, savory, and garlicky goodness. Originating from the bustling street food scene of South Korea, Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean style has taken the world by storm, captivating foodies with its unique texture and irresistible flavor profile. This isn't your average garlic bread; it's a gourmet experience that combines the comforting warmth of freshly baked bread with a rich, creamy filling and an aromatic garlic butter glaze. If you've been searching for a show-stopping bread recipe that's both easy to make and incredibly satisfying, look no further. This article will guide you through everything you need to know about crafting the perfect Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread in your own kitchen.

## What is Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread?

Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread, often simply called "Korean Garlic Bread" or "6-piece Garlic Bread" due to its distinctive cuts, is a sweet and savory bread roll that has become a viral sensation. Unlike traditional Italian garlic bread, which is typically a savory side dish, the Korean version elevates the concept into a more indulgent treat that can be enjoyed as a snack, appetizer, or even a light meal. It starts with a soft, often brioche-like or milk bread roll, which is deeply cut into six or eight wedges, but not all the way through, creating a flower-like shape.

These crevices are then generously filled with a sweetened cream cheese mixture. The entire bread is then dipped into or brushed with a decadent garlic butter glaze, which often includes a touch of sugar and sometimes condensed milk, giving it a signature glossy finish and a balanced sweet-savory punch. The result is a bread that boasts a crispy, golden-brown exterior, a moist and fluffy interior, and a burst of creamy, garlicky, and slightly sweet flavor in every bite. It’s a testament to the innovative spirit of Korean baking, blending familiar ingredients into something truly extraordinary.

## Ingredients for Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread

Crafting this delightful bread requires a few key components. The beauty lies in the simplicity of the ingredients, which come together to create a complex flavor profile.

For the Bread:
*   6 soft bread rolls (brioche buns, milk bread buns, or a similar soft round roll work best)

For the Cream Cheese Filling:
*   200g cream cheese, softened at room temperature
*   50g granulated sugar (adjust to your sweetness preference)
*   1/4 teaspoon salt
*   1-2 tablespoons milk (optional, for smoother consistency)

For the Garlic Butter Glaze:
*   100g unsalted butter, melted
*   4-6 cloves garlic, finely minced (or 2-3 tablespoons pre-minced garlic)
*   30g granulated sugar (again, adjust to taste)
*   1/4 teaspoon salt
*   1 tablespoon dried parsley flakes (or 2 tablespoons fresh, chopped)
*   1 large egg, whisked (for an optional egg wash, to help bind the glaze)
*   1-2 tablespoons condensed milk (optional, for extra sweetness and gloss)

Optional Garnish:
*   Fresh chopped parsley
*   Parmesan cheese
*   More condensed milk for drizzling

## Step-by-Step Guide to Making Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread

Making Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread is a fun and rewarding process. Follow these steps for a perfect batch:

1.  **Prepare the Bread:** Preheat your oven to 160°C (325°F). Take your soft bread rolls and, using a sharp knife, make 6-8 deep cuts around the circumference of each roll, radiating from the center like spokes on a wheel. Be careful not to cut all the way through the base of the bread; you want the bottom to remain intact so the bread holds its shape.

2.  **Make the Cream Cheese Filling:** In a medium bowl, combine the softened cream cheese, granulated sugar, and salt. Mix until thoroughly combined and smooth. If the mixture is too thick, add a tablespoon or two of milk to achieve a pipeable consistency. Transfer the cream cheese filling into a piping bag fitted with a round nozzle (or a zip-top bag with a corner snipped off). Pipe the cream cheese generously into each cut of the bread roll.

3.  **Prepare the Garlic Butter Glaze:** In a separate bowl, melt the unsalted butter. Add the finely minced garlic, granulated sugar, salt, and dried parsley flakes. Whisk in the whisked egg and optional condensed milk until well combined. This creates a rich, aromatic, and slightly emulsified glaze.

4.  **Glaze the Bread:** There are two methods for this. You can either gently dip each bread roll, top-side down, into the garlic butter glaze, ensuring all the cuts and the top surface are thoroughly coated. Alternatively, place the bread rolls on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, and use a pastry brush to generously brush the garlic butter glaze over and into every crevice of the bread. Don't be shy with the glaze; it's what gives the bread its characteristic flavor and moisture.

5.  **Bake the Bread:** Place the glazed bread rolls on the parchment-lined baking sheet. Bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until the bread is golden brown on the outside and the cream cheese filling is warm and slightly bubbly. Keep an eye on them to prevent burning.

6.  **Serve and Garnish:** Once baked, remove the Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread from the oven. For an extra flourish, sprinkle with fresh chopped parsley, a dusting of Parmesan cheese, or a drizzle of extra condensed milk while still warm. Serve immediately and enjoy the incredible blend of flavors and textures.

## Tips for Perfect Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread

Achieving that perfect balance of crispy, creamy, and flavorful isn't hard, but a few tips can elevate your homemade Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread:

*   **Quality Bread is Key:** Start with the softest, freshest bread rolls you can find. Brioche or milk bread are ideal for their rich flavor and tender crumb, which perfectly complements the fillings.
*   **Room Temperature Cream Cheese:** Ensure your cream cheese is fully softened. This makes it much easier to mix into a smooth, lump-free filling and pipe neatly into the bread.
*   **Don't Skimp on the Garlic Butter:** The garlic butter glaze is the heart of this dish. Be generous when brushing or dipping the bread. The bread will absorb a good amount, keeping it moist and flavorful.
*   **Adjust Sweetness to Taste:** The original Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread can be quite sweet. Feel free to adjust the amount of sugar in both the cream cheese filling and the garlic butter glaze to suit your preference.
*   **Fresh Garlic for Best Flavor:** While pre-minced garlic works in a pinch, fresh garlic cloves, finely minced, will provide a more pungent and aromatic flavor to your glaze.
*   **Proper Cutting Technique:** The cuts should be deep enough to hold a good amount of cream cheese but not so deep that the bread falls apart. Practice on one roll if you're unsure.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings

Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread is incredibly versatile. It's best enjoyed warm, straight out of the oven, when the bread is soft, the cream cheese is gooey, and the garlic butter is fragrant.

*   **As a Snack or Appetizer:** Its individual portion size makes it perfect for sharing or as a satisfying snack on its own.
*   **Brunch Item:** Serve it alongside eggs, bacon, or fresh fruit for a unique brunch experience.
*   **Dessert:** Believe it or not, with its sweet elements, it can also function as a light dessert, especially when drizzled with extra condensed milk.
*   **Pairings:** It pairs wonderfully with a cup of coffee, milk tea, or even a glass of cold milk. For a savory twist, try it with a light vegetable soup or a simple green salad to balance the richness.

## Variations and FAQs

**Variations:**
*   **Spicy Kick:** Add a pinch of red pepper flakes to your garlic butter glaze for a subtle heat.
*   **Herby Cream Cheese:** Incorporate finely chopped chives or other herbs into your cream cheese filling for an extra layer of flavor.
*   **Cheesy Topping:** Sprinkle some mozzarella or Parmesan cheese on top of the bread before baking for a gooey, cheesy crust.
*   **Savory Only:** If you prefer a less sweet version, reduce or omit the sugar from both the cream cheese and garlic butter.

**FAQs:**
*   **Can I make Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread ahead of time?** You can prepare the bread by making the cuts and preparing both the cream cheese filling and garlic butter glaze a day in advance. Store the filling and glaze separately in the refrigerator. Assemble and bake just before serving for the best results.
*   **What if I don't have a piping bag?** A zip-top bag with one corner snipped off works perfectly as a makeshift piping bag for the cream cheese filling.
*   **Can I use frozen bread rolls?** Yes, if you thaw them completely first. Make sure they are soft before cutting.
*   **Is it very sweet?** The traditional version can be quite sweet due to the sugar in both the cream cheese and garlic butter, sometimes with added condensed milk. However, you can easily adjust the sugar content to your personal preference.
*   **How do I store leftovers?** Store any leftover Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 days, or in the refrigerator for up to 4 days. Reheat gently in a toaster oven or microwave.

This delightful Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread is more than just a recipe; it's an experience. Its unique blend of flavors and textures makes it an unforgettable treat that's sure to impress anyone who tries it. Whether you're a seasoned baker or a curious beginner, this recipe offers a delicious journey into the heart of Korean baking. So roll up your sleeves, gather your ingredients, and get ready to create a masterpiece that will have everyone asking for more!
